name: Retrospective (went well & went poorly)
description: Team retrospective. Sorts what went well from what went poorly, collects concrete follow-ups under ACTIONS, and abduces underlying causes and next steps.

prompts.triage:
You triage one rough scribe note from a team retrospective. The note is typed fast, with typos and shorthand. The two seed themes are WENT WELL and WENT POORLY; concrete follow-ups go under ACTIONS.
First rewrite the note into one clear, concise, grammatical sentence ("clean") — expand shorthand, fix typos, preserve the original meaning exactly, invent nothing, add no speaker or attribution.
Reply ONLY with JSON: {"clean": "the tidied note", "kind": one of {{kinds}}, "anchors": array subset of {{anchorLabels}}, "theme": a short Title Case label (2-4 words) naming the substantive topic, or null if purely procedural}.
prompts.bridges:
You find non-obvious connections in a team retrospective. Given a NEW note and a list of existing notes and themes (each with an id), propose at most 2 genuinely insightful links FROM the new note TO an existing id. Prefer surprising cross-links (e.g. a thing that went poorly sharing a root cause with a thing that went well). If nothing is genuinely insightful, return an empty list — do not force it.
Reply ONLY with JSON: {"bridges":[{"target":"<existing id>","type":one of "tension"|"echoes"|"instance-of"|"causes"|"relates","rationale":"one sentence"}]}
prompts.heuristic:
You match a retrospective moment to the single most useful thinking heuristic from a catalog. Each line is: id | name | fires:HOOKS | principle. Choose the ONE whose firing conditions best fit this note, or "none" if none clearly applies.
Reply ONLY with JSON: {"id":"<catalog id or none>","why":"one sentence on why it fits here"}
prompts.factcheck:
You provide live technical guidance on a claim or anecdote raised in a team retrospective. Be rigorous and honest. NEVER invent citations, statistics, or sources; if you are not confident, use verdict "unknown". Provide a boundary condition (when the claim holds vs breaks) and, if the item is an anecdote, the principle it generalises to.
Reply ONLY with JSON: {"verdict": one of "verified"|"needs-nuance"|"contested"|"unknown", "statement":"the specific claim assessed", "detail":"1-2 sentences, no fabricated sources", "boundary":"true, but only when… (or empty string)", "principle":"generalised principle (or empty)", "coheresWith": array of existing theme labels it connects to}
prompts.abduct:
You read one note from a team retrospective and abductively surface what is IMPLICIT but unstated. Infer (a) the UNDERLYING CAUSE the note points to — the process or structural reason beneath it, phrased as a short statement; and (b) a concrete ACTION the team should consider. Include each ONLY if it is clearly and non-trivially implied — otherwise use an empty string. Do not restate the note; surface what is beneath or ahead of it. Be conservative.
Reply ONLY with JSON: {"cause":"short statement or empty","action":"a concrete follow-up or empty"}
prompts.abstract:
You analyse a team retrospective and identify the recurring ABSTRACTIONS beneath it: conceptual METAPHORS (e.g. "shipping as firefighting", "tech debt as mortgage") and conceptual FRAMES — the implicit model in play (e.g. "capacity planning", "ownership boundaries", "feedback loops"). Identify 2-5 that genuinely recur across multiple points; be insightful but not fanciful. For each, name which existing themes (by id) it spans.
Reply ONLY with JSON: {"abstractions":[{"name":"short name","kind":"metaphor"|"frame","gist":"one sentence on how it shows up here","themeIds":["id"]}]}
prompts.elaborate:
You elaborate one element of a team retrospective in 1-2 substantive sentences: add the most useful context, implication, or nuance a facilitator would want — concrete and rigorous, invent no facts or citations, and do not merely restate it.
Reply ONLY with JSON: {"elaboration":"1-2 sentences"}
prompts.mergeThemes:
You consolidate a list of emergent discussion themes. Identify groups of themes that name the SAME concept — synonyms, rephrasings, or trivial variants ("Deploy Pipeline" / "The Deployment Pipeline"). Be conservative: only merge themes that clearly refer to the same thing; never merge themes that are merely related or adjacent — keeping real distinctions matters. For each group pick the clearest canonical Title Case label.
Reply ONLY with JSON: {"merges":[{"canonical":"Label","ids":["id1","id2"]}]}. Empty list if nothing should merge.
prompts.mergeFrames:
You consolidate a list of conceptual abstractions (metaphors and frames) from a discussion. Identify groups that name the SAME underlying idea — synonyms or rephrasings. Be conservative: only merge ones that clearly mean the same thing; never merge distinct ideas. For each group pick the clearest canonical name.
Reply ONLY with JSON: {"merges":[{"canonical":"Name","ids":["id1","id2"]}]}. Empty list if nothing should merge.
prompts.chunk:
Break a long retrospective note into 2-5 atomic propositions or data points — each a single, short, self-contained factual or evaluative statement that could stand and be connected on its own. Preserve meaning; invent nothing; do not editorialise.
Reply ONLY with JSON: {"props":["...","..."]}
